purpose: Quantifying how interest rate differentials drive FX movements conditional on the volatility regime.
components: Low-vol regime: 8-12% R-squared between rate differentials and FX
Structural break confirmed by Chow test F=7.2
Maps to Dollar Smile middle zone (low-vol) vs left/right tails (high-vol)
Carry-to-vol deception: nominal carry overstates risk-adjusted return in low-vol
current_assessment: Most empirically robust finding inherited from prior iterations. Load-bearing across all agents frameworks. Practical utility limited by inability to identify vol regime in advance. Valid qualification but not a refutation of empirical finding.
limitations: Vol regime identification in advance is hard, limiting practical utility
Possible circularity with Dollar Smile mapping
R-squared ranges themselves may be regime-variant over longer horizons
Does not specify transition dynamics between vol regimes
